title: Cloud stocks falter as Datadog trims 2023 revenue expectations
text: Datadog Trims 2023 Revenue Expectations. Guidance Reduction: In August 2023 Datadog trimmed its full-year revenue expectations to between 2.05 billion and 2.06 billion down from the range of 2.08 billion to 2.10 billion that it provided in May. Market Context: This guidance trim reflected broader cloud software sector challenges as companies faced continued customer optimization efforts. Company Outlook: Looking forward from early 2023 Datadog planned to grow its operating expenses in fiscal year 2023 in the low 30s percent range year over year. Headcount Plans: Headcount growth targets were in the mid-20 percent range for 2023 compared to approximately 50 percent year-over-year growth in 2022. Long-term Growth: Looking forward from 2024 revenue was forecast to grow 19 percent per annum on average during the next 3 years compared to a 13 percent growth forecast for the Software industry in the US. Growth Deceleration: Datadog's annual revenue growth rate decelerated throughout 2022 peaking at 83 percent in Q1 and then declining to 74 percent in Q2 61 percent in Q3 and reaching 44 percent in Q4.
